titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Duke Ellington Oral History
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday July 12, 2019 at 00:25

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Duke Ellington Oral History is comprises ninety-two interviews with and about Ellington, one of America's greatest composers. Included among the interviewees are musicians, family members, friends, and colleagues.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: OHIII, Duke Ellington Oral History, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Streaming access to sound recordings in this collection is available when connected to the Yale network, or otherwise by request.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for OHAM materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast OHAM materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.

scopecontent.head: Interview Location
scopecontent.p: Washington, D.C. and Los Angeles, CA

scopecontent.head: Interview Location
scopecontent.p: MA Elma Lewis School of Fine Arts, Roxbury, MA

did.unittitle: Nelson, Dean Philip, WIlie Ruff, Duke Ellington
Nelson, Dean Philip, WIlie Ruff, Duke Ellington
